content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

July 18, 1991

Barry Fadem  
Bagatelos & Fadem  
The International Building  
601 California Street, Ste. 1201  
San Francisco, CA 94106

Re: Your Request for Confirmation Of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. I-91-314

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because you have not identified the persons on whose behalf you are requesting advice, we consider your request to be one for informal advice.2/

Your letter, dated June 17, 1991, correctly summarizes my advice that a ballot measure committee which is sponsored by three trade associations representing the life and health insurance industries will satisfy the provisions of Regulation 18419(b)(1) by using the name "Consumer Health Insurance Coalition, Comprised of Health and Life Insurance Associations."

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

__________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c)(3).)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
